title: Glue ear follow-up
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports concerns and functional impact, hearing difficulty, speech or behaviour context, balance, URTI and AOM history, mouth breathing, adenoid context, otoscopy, developmental assessment, hearing testing, and tympanometry.
examination.rationale: The exact paediatric OME section supports interval hearing, speech, behaviour, balance, ear discomfort, functional impact, associated or negative features, ENT examination, hearing testing, and tympanometry without independently diagnosing persistent glue ear.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports concerns and functional impact, hearing difficulty, speech or behaviour context, balance, URTI and AOM history, mouth breathing, adenoid context, otoscopy, developmental assessment, hearing testing, and tympanometry.

source.title: Otitis media with effusion in under 12s — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.2.7 — concerns, impact, recognition, and formal assessment

treatment_and_management.text: Supports clinician-arranged reassessment, interval hearing impact, changing needs, and documented questions without generating an interval or intervention.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact reassessment section supports child or caregiver concerns, clinician-entered review, arranged follow-up, and questions without generating treatment or an interval.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-arranged reassessment, interval hearing impact, changing needs, and documented questions without generating an interval or intervention.

source_limitations: Age, laterality, duration, hearing test, tympanometry, language, education, behaviour, balance, examination, diagnosis, intervention, procedure, and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
